## Metadata
- File path: package.json
- File name: package.json
- Extension: .json
- Inferred language/type: JSON package manifest for a Node.js/CommonJS TypeScript project
- Content hash: 542886390206657eb93168ffaf51442f
- Line count: 52
- Byte size: 1329
- Summary format: structured-functional-v1
- Notable symbols/classes/functions: name, version, description, license, author, homepage, bugs, repository, type, main, bin, files, engines, scripts, dependencies, devDependencies
- External dependencies: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, commander, ignore, openai, simple-git, vectra, zod, @types/node, typescript, zod-to-json-schema
- Internal dependencies: dist/index.js, dist/mcp.js, dist/scripts/generate-schemas.js, schemas, README.md, LICENSE, .diffdocrc.example
- Public API/exports: CLI executables `diffdoc` and `diffdoc-mcp`; package main entry `dist/index.js`

## Purpose
Defines the package metadata, runtime requirements, published files, CLI entry points, and dependency set for the `diffdoc` Node.js project. It also declares the build, schema-generation, clean, and start scripts used to develop and run the tool.

## User-Visible Behavior
Users installing the package get two command-line executables: `diffdoc` and `diffdoc-mcp`. Running the package’s scripts will compile TypeScript, generate schemas, start the app, or remove the build output. Package consumers also see the project name, version, description, repository links, and Node engine requirement.

## Business Rules
The package requires Node.js version 22 or newer.
The package is configured as CommonJS via `"type": "commonjs"`.
The published package is limited to the `dist`, `schemas`, `README.md`, `LICENSE`, and `.diffdocrc.example` paths.
The primary CLI entry point is `dist/index.js`, while `dist/mcp.js` is exposed as a separate command.
Build and start flows both compile TypeScript before executing generated JavaScript.
Schema generation is performed by a dedicated script in `dist/scripts/generate-schemas.js`.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
Inputs:
- Package metadata fields in `package.json`
- Source/build artifacts under `dist`
- Schema artifacts under `schemas`
- TypeScript source compiled by `tsc`
- Runtime dependencies listed in `dependencies`
- Development tooling listed in `devDependencies`

Outputs:
- Installed package metadata and CLI command registrations
- Built JavaScript in `dist`
- Generated schemas via `generate-schemas.js`
- Cleaned `dist` directory when `clean` runs

## Side Effects
- Registers executable commands in the npm package bin map.
- Compiles TypeScript during `build` and `start`.
- Generates schema files during `build` and `generate:schemas`.
- Deletes the `dist` directory during `clean`.
- Influences what files are included when the package is published.

## Error And Edge Cases
If Node.js is older than 22, the package declares itself unsupported.
If `dist/index.js` or `dist/mcp.js` is missing, the declared CLI commands will fail at runtime.
If TypeScript compilation fails, `build` and `start` will not proceed to execution.
If schema generation fails, `build` will fail after compilation.
If the published files are absent or incomplete, consumers may receive an unusable package.

## Dependencies
External dependencies:
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` for MCP-related functionality
- `commander` for CLI parsing
- `ignore` for ignore-pattern handling
- `openai` for model/API integration
- `simple-git` for git repository interaction
- `vectra` for vector storage/indexing
- `zod` for schema validation
- `typescript` and `@types/node` for development/build tooling
- `zod-to-json-schema` for schema conversion

Internal dependencies:
- Generated runtime entry points in `dist/index.js` and `dist/mcp.js`
- Schema generation script in `dist/scripts/generate-schemas.js`

## Operational Notes
This manifest is the package’s central deployment contract: changing `main`, `bin`, `files`, or `engines` directly affects installation and runtime behavior.
The `build` script is a two-step process, so schema generation depends on successful TypeScript compilation.
The `clean` script force-removes `dist`, making it safe for reset workflows but destructive to generated artifacts.
Because the package ships compiled output from `dist`, releases depend on a prior build step rather than raw source files.
The package is configured for CommonJS consumers, so module format changes would require coordinated updates to the build output and entry points.
